visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a woman standing in a brightly lit Japanese convenience store. She wears a thick, beige faux fur coat and has a look of exaggerated surprise on her face. Behind her, refrigerated shelves are filled with various pre-packaged meals and drinks. (2) Chronological montage of her shopping: a close-up shows her hand picking up a mochi cream puff from the shelf, followed by a Hokkaido milk roll cake, a small pudding cup, a can of Sapporo Classic beer, and a natto rice roll. (3) The scene transitions to a brightly lit indoor room, possibly a hotel. The woman, now in a grey zip-up hoodie, is seen from a medium close-up perspective. She holds the mochi cream puff, tears open the plastic wrapper with a crinkling sound, and shows the soft, white texture to the camera. (4) She takes a large bite of the puff, revealing a rich cream filling inside. Her facial expression is one of intense delight and shock at the taste. (5) She then shows a package of Glica Friend Bakery chocolate biscuits, takes one out, and eats it. (6) Next, she unwraps the natto rice roll, pulling the white rice cylinder out of its plastic sleeve to reveal the stringy natto beans inside. She eats it with visible enjoyment, smiling and nodding. (7) The video concludes with a close-up shot of the milk roll cake slices and a final shot of the Sapporo beer can held against a plain wall.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Subtitle]: 只有韓國人在日本🇯🇵便利商店會買這些？ (2) [Subtitle]: 1. 麻糬奶乳捲 (3) [Subtitle]: 2. 北海道生乳蛋糕 (4) [Subtitle]: 3. 布丁 (5) [Subtitle]: 4. 北海道限定啤酒 (6) [Subtitle]: 5. 納豆飯捲 (7) [Subtitle]: (只剩一個好奇購買) (8) [Subtitle]: 韓國朋友們超級推薦的😋 (9) [Subtitle]: 外層烤得像麵包一樣呢🍞 (10) [Variety]: 瘋了 (11) [Subtitle]: 先說裡面的奶油真的瘋掉 (12) [Subtitle]: 外面也不是普通的麵包 (13) [Subtitle]: 口感有點像麻糬那樣QQ的 (14) [Subtitle]: 真的要親自吃過才懂 (15) [Variety]: 請注意 (16) [Subtitle]: 這個... visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator featured throughout the video.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with long brown hair, wearing a faux fur coat initially and then a grey hoodie during the tasting segment.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: First scene is inside a convenience store (likely Lawson) with refrigerated shelves and various packaged foods. Second scene is a simple indoor room, likely a hotel room, with a bed and warm lighting.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) High-energy, major-key electronic pop BGM begins immediately. A female voice with an excited, high-arousal tone speaks in Korean, introducing the shopping trip. (2) Frequent rustling sounds of plastic packaging are heard as items are selected in the store. The voice maintains a fast, enthusiastic pace. (3) A loud, sharp crinkle signifies the opening of a mochi snack bag. This is followed by a soft squishing sound and a vocal 'Woah!' expressing amazement. (4) Distinct chewing and crunching sounds are layered over the continuous BGM as the creator tastes the snacks. Her vocal tone shifts to high-pitched delight. (5) Audible slurping and sticky 'stretching' sounds occur when the natto rice roll is consumed, accompanied by a satisfied 'Mmm' vocalization. (6) The BGM continues steadily to the end with final cheerful remarks from the speaker.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Introduction of items -> Detailed review/tasting -> Conclusion
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a travel food guide and review, specifically targeting Korean travelers in Japan by recommending 'must-buy' items from Japanese convenience stores. It uses a high-energy vlog style to showcase specific products and the creator's emotional reaction to their taste.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Prominent display and positive review of Lawson's 'Uchi Cafe' products and Sapporo beer.

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video effectively uses social media tropes like high-energy reactions and text-heavy overlays to maintain viewer engagement.
